purpose: Predeclare the controlled local S1/F-N/D-R comparison without running it or advancing a collaboration claim.

conductor_may_not: mint identity, membership, leases, grants, receipts, or other authority
mutate owner records or Agentgres truth directly
become a scheduler, runtime, truth owner, or shared authority plane

prerequisites: one readiness verifier passes all five P0 readiness conditions
a P0 readiness manifest records a go recommendation
the task manifest, eligible-path snapshot, local cost schedule, rubric release, and arm-order schedule are content-hash sealed before the first arm starts
stop_rules: Do not activate from a contract, unit, integration, readiness, or protocol-check command.
Do not run any claim-bearing arm while this artifact remains frozen_not_activated.
Any protocol change after the first observed arm output invalidates this cohort rather than tuning it.

task_admission.selection_rule: Select twelve previously unobserved local repository tasks satisfying the three frozen strata; seal exact task bodies, bases, fixtures, rubrics, and hashes before any arm output is observed.
task_admission.replacement_rule: No task is dropped, replaced, or relabeled after any arm begins; invalid, failed, collapsed, and inconclusive tasks remain in intention-to-treat results.

arm_order.assignment: Sort sealed task refs lexically and assign the three orders cyclically; do not rerandomize from outcomes.

arms.definition: The strongest complete direct GoalRun with unified context.
arms.context_topology: one unified context visible only through the admitted direct GoalRun path
arms.path_selection: Choose the strongest eligible complete direct path from the sealed eligibility snapshot before cohort activation.
arms.shared_state: none beyond the direct GoalRun owner state

arms.definition: The strongest explicitly declared fixed-N GoalRun topology.
arms.path_selection: Choose the two strongest distinct eligible complete implementer paths from the sealed eligibility snapshot before cohort activation; N remains two for every task.
arms.shared_state: no room; isolated candidate contexts reconcile only through the GoalRun owner path

arms.definition: Begin from one GoalRun and use one hosted OutcomeRoom only through bounded room children and owner APIs.
arms.participant_paths: Use the same two sealed eligible contributor paths selected for F-N.
arms.collapse_path: On the first terminal room unavailability or integrity failure, revoke/release room participation and claims through owner APIs, retain the failure, and continue only through the original direct GoalRun. The task remains classified as D-R collapsed and is never relabeled S1.
arms.shared_state: exact-revision room graph only; no shared mutable canonical workspace, runtime ownership, or ambient authority

matched_resources.allocation_rule: All orchestration, implementer, room-coordination, recovery, and direct-GoalRun-collapse consumption shares the task execution cap. The held-out verifier cap is separate but identical across arms; all consumption from both caps enters actual cost.
matched_resources.tools_rule: The task-capability tool set is identical across arms. D-R room APIs and F-N topology APIs are coordination-only structural calls and cannot add task capability or effect authority.
matched_resources.model_rule: All selected paths come from one content-hash-sealed eligible model/harness snapshot. No arm may substitute a model or tool after observing another arm's output.
matched_resources.budget_exhaustion_rule: Stop new work at the cap, retain all partial evidence, and classify the run failed or inconclusive according to the frozen rubric. Do not grant a top-up.

critical_failures: missing daemon/Agentgres lineage required by the selected profile
outcome acceptance authored by conductor or participant
acceptance_rubric.acceptance_owner_rule: The held-out verifier emits evidence and a recommendation through existing APIs; only the owning acceptance path may admit accepted outcome truth.

required_measures: input and output tokens by invocation
model and harness identity and revision
energy estimate or explicit unavailable marker
local price-schedule revision and derived microusd
conductor, participant, verifier, retry, recovery, and collapse costs
actual_cost_accounting.price_schedule_rule: Seal one local resource price schedule before activation and apply it unchanged to all arms.
actual_cost_accounting.missing_measure_rule: A missing required cost measure invalidates economic comparison for that run but never removes the run or its other evidence.
actual_cost_accounting.zero_cost_rule: Zero monetary API spend is not zero cost; local compute and orchestration remain accounted.

failure.rule: Every unauthorized, stale, duplicate, revoked, failed, timeout, and inconclusive attempt is retained and included in intention-to-treat denominators.
failure.retry_policy: No claim-bearing retry may replace a run. A transport retry is allowed only when daemon truth proves zero model invocation and zero effect; the original attempt and retry cost remain linked.

collapse.rule: D-R collapse is triggered by a terminal room lifecycle/integrity failure or inability to renew a required bounded lease. Collapse uses owner APIs, preserves the original GoalRun, releases claims, retains room evidence, and never changes arm label.

privacy.rule: Use the same information-flow, egress, secret, retention, and tenant boundary in every arm. No arm may read another arm's outputs, traces, memory, or verifier findings before all task executions are sealed.
privacy.hard_stop: Any confirmed cross-arm contamination or forbidden disclosure makes the cohort ineligible for a positive claim.

independence.rule: The held-out verifier path is not an implementer or room participant, receives no participant authority, cannot write the candidate workspace, and cannot be selected or modified from observed arm results.

reliability.rule: Report all twelve matched tasks per arm, all attempts, completion/collapse/recovery rates, and missing evidence. No outlier deletion, best-of retry selection, or silent replacement is permitted.

economic.rule: Enforce matched hard caps and report total cost, cost per task, and cost per accepted outcome with failed, verifier, coordination, recovery, and collapse costs included.

analysis_plan.primary_endpoint: owner-admitted accepted outcomes out of twelve
analysis_plan.paired_reporting: Report task-level D-R minus S1 and D-R minus F-N differences and exact arm totals. Do not pool away task identity.
positive_local_signal_requires: all hard privacy, authority, duplication, independence, and evidence-completeness guardrails pass
D-R accepted-outcome count is not lower than either control
D-R median rubric score is no more than five points below the stronger control
D-R actual cost per accepted outcome is no more than 1.5 times the stronger control
at least four D-R tasks have owner-admitted accepted outcomes with separately attributed useful evidence from both admitted participants
no more than one D-R task collapses to the direct GoalRun path
analysis_plan.inconclusive_rule: Any missing required comparison evidence, mixed threshold result, or unresolved acceptance/challenge state is reported as inconclusive rather than tuned, imputed, or promoted.

required_truth_sources: Agentgres operations, heads, roots, projections, and replay
content-bound task, model/tool, budget, rubric, and cost-schedule manifests

nonclaims: This protocol is evidence, not a canonical architecture object or runtime contract.
This protocol does not activate an experiment, mint authority, schedule work, or accept outcomes.
The current worktree is not certified M3, M4, M5, or P0 ready by this artifact.
M9 remains the owner of terminal matched qualification, accepted-outcome and actual-cost evidence, recovery/export proof, and supported claim level.
Live Akash and qualified self-hosted weights remain later replication inputs after a controlled local result.
Multi-node continuity and useful-distribution claims remain gated by M10 and M11.
